evidence: Jacob Steeves / @const_reborn said “we upgraded the chain today” and linked the Subtensor release: https://x.com/i/status/2069229442522599847; OpenTensor promoted the protocol-upgrade discussion: https://x.com/opentensor/status/2069443585586684328
direct_impact: Replaces prior flow/netflow averages with root_prop × price × (1 − miner_burn), making active mining/IM a pass-fail gate for emissions.
second_order: TAO/alpha incentives should rotate away from inactive or self-cycling subnets toward active utility subnets; Chutes/Lium/Targon-style compute/inference subnets can benefit if they keep proving work.
watch_next: Watch TAOFlows/TaoStats/IntoTAO for subnets losing or regaining emissions, validator review cadence, miner-burn flags, and whether the formula creates new gaming vectors.

catalyst: Bittensor EVM and vTAO bridge/liquidity rails
catalyst_date_label: 2026 (already live)
evidence: Official docs cover TAO movement between Substrate and Bittensor EVM plus vTAO bridging and Aerodrome liquidity: https://docs.learnbittensor.org/evm-tutorials/bridge-vtao and https://docs.learnbittensor.org/evm-tutorials/vtao-liquidity-on-aerodrome
direct_impact: Expands TAO liquidity and composability into EVM venues.
second_order: More EVM liquidity can improve access, DeFi integrations, and staking/alpha strategies but adds bridge/liquidity risk.
watch_next: Watch bridge volumes, vTAO liquidity depth, Aerodrome pool incentives, bridge incidents, and major EVM app integrations.

catalyst: Crowdloans for subnet funding
catalyst_date_label: June 2026 (completed / already live)
evidence: Official docs describe crowdloans for funding subnet registration or extrinsics and sharing emissions by contribution: https://docs.learnbittensor.org/subnets/crowdloans
direct_impact: Lowers upfront capital burden for subnet creators and distributes exposure to new subnets.
second_order: Could increase subnet formation and speculative alpha markets; also raises quality-control risk.
watch_next: Watch crowdloan usage, failed/successful raises, contributor protections, and subnet post-launch emissions.

catalyst: Dynamic TAO governance/mainnet foundation
catalyst_date_label: January 2025 (completed)
evidence: OpenTensor announced dTAO testnet, governance passage, and first mainnet blocks: https://x.com/i/status/1877771083484410181, https://x.com/opentensor/status/1888291457124966571, and https://x.com/opentensor/status/1890879222173835269
direct_impact: Makes subnet-specific alpha markets central to TAO emissions and staking decisions.
second_order: Sustained alpha-market activity can make TAO more of an index into subnet performance.
watch_next: Watch alpha liquidity, subnet price discovery, validator behavior, and follow-on emissions reforms.

catalyst: Burn Mint Equilibrium live on mainnet
catalyst_date_label: 2026-03-23 (already live)
evidence: Akash Q1 2026 report says BME went live Mar. 23, 2026 after Proposal 318; roadmap lists AEP-76 completed: https://akash.network/blog/akash-network-q1-2026-report/ and https://akash.network/roadmap/2026/
direct_impact: Creates direct link between compute demand and AKT via burn/mint economics while preserving stable pricing UX.
second_order: If compute spend grows, AKT capture narrative improves versus USDC-only marketplace usage.
watch_next: Watch compute spend, AKT burned/minted, USDC vs AKT payment mix, provider receipts, and governance parameter changes.

catalyst: Akash crossed $5M all-time compute spend and Q1 usage milestones
evidence: Akash Q1 2026 report says first 90 days crossed an all-time high of $5M in compute spend: https://akash.network/blog/akash-network-q1-2026-report/
direct_impact: Provides KPI evidence that marketplace demand is growing.
second_order: Higher spend improves credibility of BME and provider incentives.
watch_next: Track quarterly compute spend, active leases, GPU utilization, customer retention, and revenue concentration.

catalyst: Homenode consumer/prosumer GPU supply expansion
catalyst_date_label: 2026-04-30 (completed)
evidence: Q1 report says Homenode beta opened signups for RTX 4090, RTX 5090, and Quadro RTX 6000 Ada; roadmap lists AEP-60 MVP completion Apr. 30, 2026: https://akash.network/blog/akash-network-q1-2026-report/ and https://akash.network/roadmap/2026/
direct_impact: Adds a new supply category beyond datacenter providers.
second_order: If reliable, Akash can scale cheaper inference capacity and broaden provider participation.
watch_next: Watch beta acceptance, supported GPUs, uptime/SLA, inference workloads, and Homenode earnings.

catalyst: Confidential computing and TEE roadmap
evidence: Akash 2026 roadmap lists AEP-83 Confidential Compute via Kata Containers estimated July 31, 2026: https://akash.network/roadmap/2026/
direct_impact: Adds CPU/GPU TEE workload support for enterprise and privacy-sensitive deployments.
second_order: Could unlock higher-value AI workloads that cannot use ordinary public decentralized compute.
watch_next: Watch AEP-83 delivery, attestation UX, provider support, customer pilots, and security reviews.

catalyst: CHIP token live for USD.AI governance and utility
catalyst_date_label: 2026-04-21 (already live)
evidence: USD.AI announced “$CHIP Is Live” and says CHIP holders can stake to govern risk, fees, staking module, and upgrades: https://usd.ai/insights/chip-is-live
direct_impact: Turns USD.AI from a credit/stablecoin product into a governable token system.
second_order: Governance decisions can directly affect collateral quality, loan pricing, revenue routing, and protocol risk.
watch_next: Watch staking participation, proposal cadence, fee routing, exchange/liquidity depth, and unlocks.

catalyst: USD.AI Foundation and DAO stewardship launched
evidence: Official post says the USD.AI Foundation launched as off-chain steward of the DAO and CHIP governs protocol parameters, curators, and ecosystem initiatives: https://usd.ai/insights/usdai-foundation-chip
direct_impact: Establishes governance structure and treasury/ecosystem coordination around CHIP.
second_order: Better governance can accelerate borrower onboarding and risk management; weak governance could impair credit quality.
watch_next: Watch foundation disclosures, DAO votes, curator approvals, treasury use, and legal/risk updates.

catalyst: Lighthouse report shows scaling loan book and revenue KPIs
evidence: USD.AI 2026 YTD Lighthouse report cites $398M TVL, $202M deployed, $30M Q2 annualized DAO ARR, $13B borrower pipeline, $817M signed term sheets, and $205M closed loans: https://usd.ai/insights/usdai-2026-ytd-report-lighthouse
direct_impact: Provides hard KPI basis for CHIP value capture via GPU-backed credit growth.
second_order: If ARR and deployed loans continue growing, CHIP governance over risk and fee surfaces becomes more economically important.
watch_next: Watch monthly ARR, deployed loans, defaults, sUSDai yield, borrower concentration, and pipeline-to-funded conversion.

catalyst: Large GPU-backed financing facilities validate borrower demand
evidence: Sharon AI facility up to $500M and Crucible $26.8M loan secured by 576 NVIDIA B300 GPUs: https://usd.ai/insights/sharon-ai-500m-gpu-financing-usdai and https://usd.ai/insights/crucible-capital-26m-gpu-backed-loan
direct_impact: Shows USD.AI can originate institutional-scale GPU-backed credit.
second_order: Larger facilities can grow protocol revenue but increase collateral, counterparty, and liquidation complexity.
watch_next: Watch drawdowns vs headline commitments, collateral verification, insurance, repayment performance, and loan concentration.

watch_next: Watch nearcore releases, governance/client upgrade dates, benchmark results, and any Nightshade 3.0 follow-up.
